page: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-2 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 8 GB of RAM. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it utilizes an Intel processor with 1 physical core and 2 threads per core under a dedicated CPU allocation model. The processor includes a cache hierarchy consisting of 48 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and 260 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and integer operations, alongside hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities but limited multi-core scaling. The instance achieves a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1684.29 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1834.0, both representing average tier performance. However, multi-core performance is constrained, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 1714.68 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 2251.0, both falling into the poor to weak categories. Memory performance is also a bottleneck, with uncached large-block read and write bandwidths measured at 19.35 GB/sec and 12.78 GB/sec respectively. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and text processing perform at average levels, whereas database operations, software compilation, and Redis SET operations show weaker relative performance.

Given its hardware profile, the n4-standard-2 offers an entry-level, cost-efficient option for workloads that do not require high memory bandwidth or intensive multi-core parallel processing. It is best suited for lightweight general-purpose applications, basic web serving, single-threaded background tasks, and small-scale development environments. It can handle basic, low-throughput text processing and minor single-threaded compression tasks, but is not recommended for database hosting, large-scale software compilation, or real-time LLM inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-2 is a general-purpose virtual machine configured with 2 vCPUs and 8 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel x86_64 processor with 1 physical core and 2 threads, it features a dedicated CPU allocation and a 260 MB L3 cache. The architecture supports AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for specialized vector and matrix operations. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1834.0, but multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are relatively weak. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs. It is qualitatively positioned as a cost-effective entry-level tier for lightweight workloads, such as basic web serving, single-threaded background jobs, and small development environments, where high-throughput multi-core processing and massive memory bandwidth are not required.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-2 is an x86_64 virtual machine with 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an Intel processor. It is designed for lightweight general-purpose workloads and basic web serving.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-2 is a general-purpose VM with 2 vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, suitable for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A balanced, entry-level virtual machine with dedicated Intel cores and advanced matrix extensions for lightweight general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 2 vCPUs and 8 GB RAM on a dedicated Intel x86_64 processor
Supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512
Average single-core performance suitable for single-threaded tasks
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for lightweight web serving, development, and background jobs
